heading: Telecommunications services for Senators; payment of costs out of contingent fund
label: 6315 Telecommunications services for Senators; payment of costs out of contingent fund
text: The Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper of the Senate shall furnish each Senator local and long-distance telecommunications services in Washington, District of Columbia, and in such Senator’s State in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Senate Committee on Rules and Administration; and the costs of such service shall be paid out of the contingent fund of the Senate from moneys made available to him for that purpose.
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 58a of this titleSection is from the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 1984.

Amendments1986— struck out “(except services for which the charge is based on the amount of time the service is used)” after “Senator’s State”.Pub. L. 99–4391985— inserted “and in such Senator’s State (except services for which the charge is based on the amount of time the service is used)”.Pub. L. 99–65

Effective Date of 1985 AmendmentAmendment by effective on first day of first calendar month beginning more than 60 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 99–65section 2 of Pub. L. 99–65section 6314 of this titleJuly 12, 1985

Payment for Telecommunications Service, , , as amended by , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–53, title I, § 5109 Stat. 517Pub. L. 104–197, title I, § 4(a)110 Stat. 2397Pub. L. 107–68, title I, § 104(a)115 Stat. 568Nov. 19, 1995Sept. 16, 1996Nov. 12, 2001“(a)Any payment for local and long distance telecommunications service provided to any user shall cover the total invoiced amount, including any amount relating to separately identified toll calls, and shall be charged to the appropriation for the fiscal year in which the underlying base service period covered by the invoice begins.“(b)As used in subsection (a), the term ‘user’ means a Senator, an Officer of the Senate, and any office, committee, or other entity the funds of which are disbursed by the Secretary of the Senate.”[, , , provided that: ]Pub. L. 107–68, title I, § 104(b)115 Stat. 568Nov. 12, 2001“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending , set out above] shall take effect on , and shall apply to base service periods beginning on or after that date.”section 5 of Pub. L. 104–53October 1, 2001[, , , provided that: ]Pub. L. 104–53October 1, 1996
